title: Sweat / Yellowing on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane for set yellowing; one home pass max for fresh
stainChemistry: Sweat on wool has two halves: fresh salt and sweat residue (mostly water-soluble), and the cumulative aluminum from antiperspirant that builds up and turns yellow under heat from the dryer or iron. Vinegar at low dilution and short dwell is the right tool for fresh sweat on wool — same as on silk. The hot-water plus alkaline plus enzyme cleaners that lift built-up yellow from cotton all damage wool: enzymes would damage the wool itself, hot water felts the fiber, alkaline cleaners felt and lock yellowing, agitation felts wet wool. Set yellow halos on wool are pro-only.
whyThisWorks: For fresh sweat on wool, cold water plus a brief diluted vinegar dab releases the residue without felting the fiber. For set yellow halos, the pro adds cold detergent plus a controlled reducing bleach — chemistry home can't match. Home limit is one careful pass on fresh; set yellow goes to a specialist. Repeat home rounds on wet wool felt the fiber more often than they lift more sweat.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): sweat is salt + sweat proteins + urea, often plus aluminum from antiperspirant. Aluminum chlorohydrate forms an aluminum-protein complex that yellows under heat and locks to wool keratin via hydrogen bonding. Pro uses cold NSD + dilute acetic acid (60-sec dwell) + sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution for set yellow.
defaultAssumption: Wool + fresh sweat = ONE careful home pass with cold + 60-sec vinegar. Wool + set yellow = pro-only. Aluminum-free deodorant + no fabric softener prevents recurrence.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Press cold water on the BACK with vertical pressure. No rubbing. Removes salt + fresh protein.
Cold NSD sponge press. No enzymes (digest wool). Suspends remaining protein.
Acid breaks aluminum-protein complex behind yellow halos AND restores wool's natural pH (~5). STRICT 60-sec ceiling. Flush cold immediately.
For set yellow halos, reducing bleach is wool-safer than peroxide (peroxide yellows wool further). Strict 60-sec dwell.
Cold final rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry.

steps: 5. Cold final rinse, press dry flat.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets aluminum-protein
NEVER alkaline cleaners — felt + lock yellow
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on wool — yellows wool further

homeSolutions.agent: Honest Answer — Fresh One Pass, Set Yellow Pro
homeSolutions.instruction: Sweat on wool has the same two cases as on silk. FRESH (today's wear) = one careful pass with cold + dilute vinegar can handle it. SET YELLOW (cumulative buildup, especially heat-exposed) = pro-only. Wool's banned-tools list eliminates almost everything cotton would use, AND repeat home rounds on wool felt the fiber. The aluminum-free-deodorant prevention path matters more on wool than treatment does.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Press-Blot from the BACK — Fresh Sweat Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place white towel under stain. Press a SECOND white cloth dampened in COLD water onto the BACK. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing. Cold ONLY — heat felts wool AND sets aluminum-protein. Skip if yellow is already set; go to step 5.
Half teaspoon Eucalan or Soak in two cups COLD water. Dip cloth, wring nearly dry, PRESS on stain. 3 minutes dwell. ONE PASS. Do NOT use OxiClean — alkaline opens wool scales AND locks yellow.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Press a cotton swab on the stain for 60 SECONDS MAX — set a timer. Flush cool immediately. Vinegar IS the right tool: breaks aluminum-protein complex AND restores wool's natural pH. 60-sec is the ceiling — wool weakens with prolonged acid.

Press wool between dry towels — stack, press, lift. No wringing. Lay flat on fresh towel, reshape damp, air-dry at room temperature out of sun. NEVER hang wet wool. NEVER apply heat — heat sets any residual aluminum-protein into permanent yellow.

homeSolutions.agent: Set Yellow Goes to Pro and Aluminum-Free Deodorant Prevents Recurrence
homeSolutions.instruction: Set yellow halos on wool need pro chemistry — reducing bleach at controlled dilution that home can't safely replicate, plus the tools that lift cumulative buildup from cotton (OxiClean, hot water) damage wool. Take to a wool specialist (call ahead). Tell them: "Sweat / antiperspirant yellowing on wool, age [weeks / months], one fresh round at home with cold + 60-sec vinegar. No enzymes, no hot water, no peroxide." PREVENTION: aluminum-free deodorant + no fabric softener for wool you want to preserve.

steps: 1. Fresh sweat = one careful pass. Set yellow = pro-only.
2. Cold water press-blot from the BACK (no rubbing).
3. One pass with Eucalan / Soak, 3 min cold.
4. Dilute vinegar 60-sec ceiling — vinegar IS the right tool.
5. Press dry between towels, lay flat air-dry.
6. Set yellow / cumulative = pro wool specialist.
7. Going forward: aluminum-free deodorant, no fabric softener.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one fresh-sweat pass. Set yellow on wool is pro-only. Repeat home rounds felt the wool.
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets aluminum-protein
NEVER enzymes / OxiClean / alkaline — felts + locks yellow
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on wool — yellows further
Vinegar 60-sec ceiling — wool weakens with prolonged acid

neverDo: Never agitate or scrub wet wool
Never hydrogen peroxide on wool — yellows further
Never dryer / iron heat on yellow wool
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
fiber bonds re-lock together irreversibly under heat
Hydrogen peroxide yellows wool — opposite of what you want
Vinegar IS the right tool for sweat-on-wool at controlled dilution

materialWarnings: Vinegar is the right tool for fresh sweat on wool
Repeat home rounds on wool = felting damage
Aluminum-free deodorant is the prevention path

escalation.when: Yellow halos already set, cumulative buildup heat-exposed (dryer / iron), or one fresh-sweat round doesn't clear it.
escalation.whatToTell: "Sweat / antiperspirant yellowing on wool, age [today / weeks / months]. One fresh round at home with cold + 60-sec dilute vinegar + [Eucalan]. No enzymes, no hot water, no peroxide. [Yellow halos visible / aluminum buildup suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead — confirm cold NSD + sodium hydrosulfite spotting capability)

contextQuestions: Fresh today, or yellow halos that have been there?
Has the wool been through a dryer / iron with sweat already?
Has fabric softener been used on this garment?
Wool type — merino / regular / felt / blend?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Aluminum-free deodorant doesn't cause this. Fabric softener + antiperspirant = cumulative-yellow factory. Heat-set yellow on wool is the hardest variant; pro success rate is moderate.
